task.title: Implement OpenAI-compatible OCR provider integration
task.objective: Replace the OCR provider stub path for configured OCR endpoints with a real bounded HTTP integration while preserving stub fallback for missing endpoint configuration.
acceptance_criteria: Use strict TDD: add tests first for OCR HTTP request behavior, response parsing, registry initialization, and env overrides.
When an enabled OCR provider has an endpoint, registry initialization must create a real OCR provider instead of the stub.
The real OCR provider must accept OpenAI-compatible vision chat/completions style endpoints and send image input as a base64 data URL with a language-aware prompt.
Support configurable model through OCR provider options.model and require it for the OpenAI-compatible path; preserve stub fallback when endpoint is absent.

Parse extracted text from standard chat completion responses and optional confidence from response JSON where available; return clear errors for empty image data, missing endpoint/model, non-2xx responses, empty choices, or empty extracted text.
Add TR_OCR_<NAME>_MODEL env override support without persisting secrets.
Keep implementation bounded: no parser changes, no frontend changes, no new external dependencies, no hardcoded secrets, no remote changes.
Run frontend validation from Hermes terminal: cd web-client && npx tsc --noEmit && npm run build. Run Go tests only if go exists; otherwise record blocked.
task.stop_condition: Stop after OCR provider integration is implemented, reviewed, validated, recorded in orchestrator state, and committed; do not wire OCR into PDF page rasterization or scanned document parsing in this slice.
validation: If go exists: go test ./internal/provider ./internal/config; otherwise record blocked
